assertionResults.title: Round-trip navigation preserves state
assertionResults.reason: INP -> LCP -> INP preserved pathname, t, n, filters param, and active filter tags, but hash fragment (#f=url) was dropped on the first navigation and remained missing after returning to INP.
reproductionSteps: Open /rum-inp.html with domain and domainkey URL params.
Set time range to Last Month (t=30d) and Top N to Top 20 (n=20).
Set hash to #f=url and capture start state.
Navigate INP -> LCP -> INP via top nav tabs.
Compare start vs end URL/DOM state.

evidence.network: agent-browser network requests returned no captured entries; fallback evidence via performance resource timings showed 62 fetch entries to bundles.aem.page (latest included /2026/04/12, /2026/04/13, /2026/04/14 endpoints).

assertionResults.issues: Expected #f=url hash to survive round-trip navigation, but destination URLs had empty hash.

frictions.description: agent-browser network requests command reported no captured entries during this run.
frictions.resolution: Used performance.getEntriesByType('resource') in page context as fallback network evidence.

summary: Tested 1 assertion (VAL-NAV-007): failed due to hash fragment loss on INP -> LCP -> INP round-trip despite preserved query params and active filter tag.
